title: Seriphidium kurramense
taxonomy: Seriphidium kurramense is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Anthemideae. This species is further classified under the genus Seriphidium. The classification of Seriphidium kurramense is also represented in its binomial nomenclature, where it is referred to as Seriphidium kurramense.

intro: Seriphidium kurramense, also known as Eupatoriopsis kurramensis, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, digestive disorders, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Seriphidium kurramense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used in traditional remedies for their medicinal value.
